evals.prompt: How do I get the newest version of the ram plugin? I heard there's a new skill I want to try out.
evals.expected_output: Walks through refreshing the marketplace catalog, asks (or infers) update scope, runs the update command, and verifies the new version.
expectations: Runs or instructs `claude plugin marketplace update ram-companies` before attempting the update
Asks whether to update at `user` or `project` scope before running the update, since the prompt doesn't specify
Runs or instructs `claude plugin update ram@ram-companies` (with `--scope project` if that's the resolved scope)
Suggests verifying the result with `claude plugin list`

evals.prompt: We just merged a PR that bumps the ram plugin to 1.6.0 on main. Update it for the whole team on this project, not just for me.
evals.expected_output: Infers project scope from 'whole team' without re-asking, and updates + verifies against version 1.6.0.
expectations: Infers project scope from the 'whole team' phrasing instead of asking a redundant scope question
Runs or instructs `claude plugin update ram@ram-companies --scope project`
Confirms the installed version now matches 1.6.0, e.g. via `claude plugin list`

Explains that project scope only commits config to git and does not push the update to teammates' machines — each teammate still needs to pull the change and run their own `claude plugin marketplace update` (or equivalent refresh) afterward

evals.prompt: I ran `claude plugin update ram@ram-companies` but it says no update is available. Did I do something wrong?
evals.expected_output: Troubleshoots the stale-catalog and not-yet-merged-version cases before assuming the command itself is broken.
expectations: Suggests re-running `claude plugin marketplace update ram-companies` to refresh the local catalog
Suggests checking whether the version bump in `plugin.json` has actually been merged to `main` yet
Does not conclude the update command is broken without first ruling out these two causes
